data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S1 6d56: Faithful, lock-free snapshot materialization core)]

VERDICT: EMPTY G3 findings list. 4-bucket audit of this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE EIGHT-child roster (S1 6d56 THIS materialize core, S2 70c7 cache read/populate, S2b b73c janitor reclamation, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence/ADR 0002, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing, S6 1ba5 docs). The union of children covers every parent criterion; no uncovered parent criterion is attributable to this pairing.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; THIS child's role + bucket):

- Parent AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as PRIMARY OWNER of the faithful-materialization half: its AC1 (materialize(ref) produces a directory byte-matching the committed tree at the resolved SHA, INCLUDING export-ignore files — faithful, not git archive), AC2 (LFS pointer detection + submodule handling defined/documented), AC3 (atomic population; reader never sees a partial tree; crashed materialization leaves only tmp/, cleaned on startup). The re-rooting half (pointing the gate file tools at the snapshot dir, never the working tree) is owned by S3 (fa49 AC1/AC3). Heart of this child. Clean.

- Parent AC2 (a signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the signed manifest channel — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures verify — reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3 AC1/AC2: verified-at-sha manifest step, no PAYLOAD_VERSION bump, reproducible re-run) atop THIS child's immutable-SHA basis (its What: "resolve ref to an immutable SHA against origin"). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child: it supplies the immutable, byte-identical materialization that MAKES reproducibility achievable but does not sign (its Scope: "Out: ... signing (S4)"). Not an absence (S4 owns the binding).

- Parent AC3 (local source mode reads the in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local is the documented back-out) — covered SPLIT: S3 (fa49 AC4) owns "reads in-place checkout" + S4 (30b3 AC4) owns "cannot sign" + S6 docs the back-out. THIS child's role is minimal but consistent: its What/AC5 says "attested mode fails closed, local mode still runs" (the materialize core's local-vs-attested fail posture), and its Scope explicitly defers signing/gate-wiring to S4/S3. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.

- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; the snapshot cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark — S2 — without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly, with THIS child OWNING the git-lock-contention + atomic-population half: its AC6 (concurrent materialize() of different SHAs do not contend on index.lock/config.lock — via the temp-index GIT_INDEX_FILE + coalesced fetch design), its AC3 (atomic dir-rename so a reader never observes a partial tree). The single-flight + delete-on-last-close reader-safety half is S2 (70c7 AC1/AC2/AC3); the watermark/LRU reclamation-without-disrupting-readers half is S2b (b73c AC1/AC6). No double-implementation; no gap.

- Parent AC5 (private-repo fetch failures surface a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as the BEHAVIORAL OWNER: its AC5 (a private-repo fetch with missing credentials raises a descriptive, actionable error; attested mode fails closed) + its What (the coalesced git fetch origin is the only lock-taking step; an auth-failed fetch surfaces an actionable credential message, not a raw git error). S6 (1ba5 AC1/AC2) owns the DOCS half (requirement + message + remedy + fail-closed/local-still-runs). Raise (this child) + document (S6) pairing complete. Heart of this child's error surface. Clean.

- Parent AC6 (portable across macOS + Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as PRIMARY/SOLE BEHAVIORAL OWNER: its AC4 (no external tar binary invoked; no hardcoded /tmp; runs on macOS + Linux) + its What (extraction never shells to external tar — uses read-tree + checkout-index; temp root from tempfile.gettempdir()/mkdtemp 0700, overridable via REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR, created OUTSIDE the repo/.git; fcntl.flock with atomic-mkdir fallback on a local FS). S6 (1ba5 AC5) documents REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + the EFS/NFS flock caveat. The cross-process flock pieces of S2 (locks/<sha>) and S2b (gc/lock) RIDE this child's portability seam without re-owning the tar/tmp/fallback sub-clauses. Heart of this child. Clean.

- Parent AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — FULLY covered by S2b (b73c AC9: watermark/grace/max-age/reverify configurable with documented defaults) + S3 (fa49 ref/source defaults via the standard rebar config + REBAR_* precedence) + S6 (docs). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. THIS child: it introduces REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR (an override, documented by S6) but does not own the watermark/ref/grace tunables. Not an absence (S2b/S3 own them).

- Parent AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives — PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — FULLY covered by S2/S2b (b73c AC10: cache/janitor architecture ADR + the rejected PID+heartbeat lease) + S4 (30b3 AC1: the manifest-vs-payload SHA-binding decision recorded in the ADR — covering the rejected payload-schema SHA-binding alternative) + S4b (e308 AC3: ADR 0002 consolidation). THIS child's novel patterns (read-tree+checkout-index over git archive, the atomic dir-rename, the portability seam) are captured by the S2/S4-authored ADR per the parent's "authored in S2/S4" note; this child need not author the ADR itself. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence (S2b/S4/S4b own the ADR clauses).

- Parent AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children in the roster (this child is S1, the riskiest-first primitive, 1 of 8) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("Every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC6. The "immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout" foundation is PRECISELY this child's core deliverable: faithful read-tree+checkout-index materialization at the resolved immutable SHA into a temp dir OUTSIDE the repo, byte-identical across runs (its AC1/AC2/AC3) — the substrate every other child builds on (S2 caches it, S2b reclaims it, S3 re-roots gates onto it, S4 signs over its SHA, S5 surfaces the ref, S6 documents it). The "client-specified" surfacing is S5; the "signed/verified_at_sha/manifest channel" binding is S4; the "unsigned local verify-before-push" clauses are S3/S4. No uncovered obligation introduced by this child's existence.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences): this child's Scope explicitly delegates each downstream piece to a named, present sibling — "Out: the cache/janitor (S2)" -> S2 70c7 + S2b b73c; "gate wiring (S3)" -> S3 fa49; "signing (S4)" -> S4 30b3; "surfaces (S5)" -> S5 b7fa. The descriptive-credential MESSAGE detail this child raises is documented by S6 (1ba5 AC2, "see S6" cross-ref bidirectionally acknowledged). The submodule-handling DOCUMENTATION (this child's AC2 says "defined and documented") lands in S6's ref/source-semantics docs. Each delegated obligation is claimed by a named sibling in the eight-story roster. Covered-by-named-consumer.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) / S4b (e308) — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check across the S3<->S4/S4b seam — is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about WHO threads the resolved-SHA handle (which THIS child produces) into the attestation hashing path, not a G3 union-coverage gap. The corresponding parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, ref-coherence delegated to S4b) remain CLAIMED by children, so the union covers them; the WHO-threads-it question lives in the G4 channel and is unrelated to this materialization-core child's own coverage.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the faithful-materialization-core slice of the parent criteria — it is the PRIMARY OWNER of parent AC1's faithful-snapshot half, parent AC5's credential-error raise, parent AC6's portability (no-tar/no-/tmp/macOS+Linux/flock+mkdir-fallback), and the atomic-population + no-index.lock/config.lock-contention half of parent AC4; it supplies the immutable-SHA basis the success-criteria reproducibility rests on. Every parent acceptance/success criterion has a named owner in the eight-story roster; none is left uncovered. NO G3 finding for this pairing.
